instructions.instruction: Before grouping, read all commits between the current branch and the base branch, then get the files changed per commit.
instructions.relevant_when: User asks to decompose a branch into smaller PRs.

instructions.instruction: Group commits into 2-4 concern buckets: Infrastructure, Application logic, Tests, Housekeeping. Adapt labels to the codebase if needed.
Present the proposed grouping as a table (PR | Branch name | Commits | Concern) for user approval BEFORE taking any git action.
Wait for explicit user confirmation before creating any branches or running any git commands.
Name branches descriptively based on actual content — NEVER use sequence numbers (wave-1, phase-2, part-1, step-3).
instructions.relevant_when: Naming branches in the decomposition proposal.

instructions.instruction: PR 1 targets the base branch. Each subsequent PR targets the previous one (stacked branches: base ← PR-1 ← PR-2 ← PR-3).
instructions.relevant_when: Creating branches for the decomposed PRs.

instructions.instruction: ALWAYS resolve cherry-pick conflicts before reporting completion. NEVER leave a branch in a conflicted state.
instructions.relevant_when: Creating stacked branches from the approved grouping.

instructions.instruction: MR title format: `<type>(<ticket>): <imperative summary of the change>`.
instructions.relevant_when: Generating MR titles for each decomposed PR.

instructions.instruction: MR description must answer What (one sentence: the diff in plain English) and Why (one sentence: motivation). It is NOT a file list or commit log.
instructions.relevant_when: Generating MR descriptions for each decomposed PR.

instructions.instruction: After creating branches, remind the user to set the correct target branch for each MR: PR 1 → base, PR 2 → PR 1's branch, PR 3 → PR 2's branch.
A commit touching both application logic and tests belongs to the group representing the majority of the change.
instructions.relevant_when: Assigning a mixed commit to a concern group.

instructions.instruction: Supporting commits (lock files, generated code) go with the group they enable.
NEVER split commits that share a single failing test. If PR 1 removes a handler and PR 2 adds its replacement, they must travel together.
instructions.relevant_when: Evaluating whether two commits can be split across PRs.

instructions.instruction: Do not decompose a branch where all commits touch the same component, all are test-only for one feature, or the work is already in review.
instructions.relevant_when: Deciding whether to proceed with decomposition.
